sections.paragraph:
& חִזְקִיָּהוּ (“the might of Jehovah,” i.e. given by Jehovah; like the Germ. Gotthard), [Hezekiah, Hizkiah, Hizkijah], pr.n. Gr. Ἐζεχίας, Lat. Ezechias, borne-

(1) by a king of Judah, 728-699 B. C., 2Ki 18:1, 10 2Ki 18:10also called יְהִזְקִיָּה and יְחִזְקִיָּהוּ for יְחֶזְּקִיָּהוּ, יְחַזְּקִיָּהוּ, in the manner of derivatives of the future, (like יְחֶזְקֵאל for יְחַזְקְאֵל), Hos 1:1 Isa 1:1.-(2) one of the ancestors of the prophet Zephaniah, whom many suppose to be the same as Hezekiah the king, Zep 1:1.-(3) 1Ch 3:23.-(4) Neh 7:21 10:18 .
